attributes.description: AWS EC2 dashboard for CloudWatch metrics collected by the OpenTelemetry Collector.
attributes.title: [AWS OTel] EC2

embeddableConfig.description:
Monitor AWS EC2 instances across your infrastructure in real time.
Use the filters at the top to narrow by instance, region, or account. Spot outliers instantly in the summary table — click any instance to filter the dashboard.
To get started, instrument your EC2 instances with the Elastic agent.
Total number of EC2 instances — distinct InstanceId values in the latest collected data for the selected time range.

attributes.description: Total number of EC2 instances — distinct InstanceId values in the latest collected data for the selected time range.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest) <= 5 | STATS total = COUNT(*) | KEEP total

embeddableConfig.title: Instances by status
embeddableConfig.description: Share of EC2 instances by latest status-check result — impaired (StatusCheckFailed > 0), healthy (= 0), or no data (metric missing). Uses the most recent value per instance within a 5-minute freshness window. A growing impaired slice indicates spreading status-check failures.

attributes.title: Instances by status (checks)
attributes.description: Share of EC2 instances by latest status-check result — impaired (StatusCheckFailed > 0), healthy (= 0), or no data (metric missing). Uses the most recent value per instance within a 5-minute freshness window. A growing impaired slice indicates spreading status-check failures.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | EVAL ec2_state = CASE(failed_recent > 0, "impaired", failed_recent == 0, "healthy", "no data") | STATS instances = COUNT(*) BY ec2_state

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | EVAL ec2_state = CASE(failed_recent > 0, "impaired", failed_recent == 0, "healthy", "no data") | STATS instances = COUNT(*) BY ec2_state

embeddableConfig.title: EC2 instance count over time
embeddableConfig.description: Count of EC2 instances over time, classified per time bucket as impaired (StatusCheckFailed > 0), healthy (= 0), or no data (metric missing). Distinct instances are counted per bucket, so totals track how many instances reported in each interval. A rising impaired series indicates spreading failures.

attributes.title: EC2 instance count over time
attributes.description: Count of EC2 instances over time, classified per time bucket as impaired (StatusCheckFailed > 0), healthy (= 0), or no data (metric missing). Distinct instances are counted per bucket, so totals track how many instances reported in each interval. A rising impaired series indicates spreading failures.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS failed_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(failed_max > 0, "impaired", failed_max == 0, "healthy", "no data") | STATS total = COUNT(*), healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy", impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "impaired", no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "no data" BY timestamp | SORT timestamp ASC

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS failed_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 50, ?_tstart, ?_tend), InstanceId, cloud.region | EVAL state = CASE(failed_max > 0, "impaired", failed_max == 0, "healthy", "no data") | STATS total = COUNT(*), healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy", impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "impaired", no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "no data" BY timestamp | SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.description: Number of impaired EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is greater than 0, within a 5-minute freshness window. A non-zero count indicates instances failing EC2 status checks and likely unreachable.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | STATS impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent > 0 | KEEP impaired

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | STATS impaired = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent > 0 | KEEP impaired

attributes.description: Number of impaired EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is greater than 0, within a 5-minute freshness window. A non-zero count indicates instances failing EC2 status checks and likely unreachable.

embeddableConfig.description: Number of healthy EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is 0, within a 5-minute freshness window.

attributes.description: Number of healthy EC2 instances — distinct instances whose latest StatusCheckFailed is 0, within a 5-minute freshness window.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | STATS healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent == 0 | KEEP healthy

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | STATS healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent == 0 | KEEP healthy

embeddableConfig.title: EC2 instances
embeddableConfig.description: All EC2 instances, ranked impaired first (worst first). Click an instance to drill into its metrics. State reflects the latest StatusCheckFailed within a 5-minute freshness window (impaired > 0, healthy = 0, otherwise no data). Max CPU is the peak utilization (%) over the selected time range; disk-write-ops and network/disk byte columns are averaged per CloudWatch period over the range.

attributes.title: EC2 instances
attributes.description: All EC2 instances, ranked impaired first (worst first). Click an instance to drill into its metrics. State reflects the latest StatusCheckFailed within a 5-minute freshness window (impaired > 0, healthy = 0, otherwise no data). Max CPU is the peak utilization (%) over the selected time range; disk-write-ops and network/disk byte columns are averaged per CloudWatch period over the range.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS c_failed_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)), c_region = MAX(cloud.region), c_cpu = MAX(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_disk_write_ops = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskWriteOps`, NULL)), c_dr = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskReadBytes`, NULL)), c_dw = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskWriteBytes`, NULL)), c_ni = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkIn`, NULL)), c_no = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`, NULL)), last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | EVAL c_cpu = COALESCE(c_cpu, 0.0), c_disk_write_ops = COALESCE(c_disk_write_ops, 0.0), c_dr = COALESCE(c_dr, 0.0), c_dw = COALESCE(c_dw, 0.0), c_ni = COALESCE(c_ni, 0.0), c_no = COALESCE(c_no, 0.0) | EVAL c_state_sort = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, 1, c_failed_max == 0, 2, 3), c_state = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, "impaired", c_failed_max == 0, "healthy", "no data") | SORT c_state_sort ASC

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | STATS c_failed_max = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)), c_region = MAX(cloud.region), c_cpu = MAX(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/CPUUtilization`, NULL)) / 100.0, c_disk_write_ops = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskWriteOps`, NULL)), c_dr = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskReadBytes`, NULL)), c_dw = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/DiskWriteBytes`, NULL)), c_ni = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkIn`, NULL)), c_no = AVG(CASE(stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/NetworkOut`, NULL)), last_seen = MAX(@timestamp) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(last_seen) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", last_seen, latest_global) <= 5 | EVAL c_cpu = COALESCE(c_cpu, 0.0), c_disk_write_ops = COALESCE(c_disk_write_ops, 0.0), c_dr = COALESCE(c_dr, 0.0), c_dw = COALESCE(c_dw, 0.0), c_ni = COALESCE(c_ni, 0.0), c_no = COALESCE(c_no, 0.0) | EVAL c_state_sort = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, 1, c_failed_max == 0, 2, 3), c_state = CASE(c_failed_max > 0, "impaired", c_failed_max == 0, "healthy", "no data") | SORT c_state_sort ASC

embeddableConfig.title: EC2 instances — hexagon map (click an hex for actions)
embeddableConfig.description: One hexagon per EC2 instance, ordered impaired then healthy then no-data (worst first). Toggle Color by between Status, CPU utilization, or EBS writes (MB per CloudWatch period); metric views support Min, Max, Avg, and Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon to filter the dashboard or open metrics for that instance.

embeddableConfig.description: Number of EC2 instances reporting metrics but with no StatusCheckFailed signal in the latest 5-minute window (shown as “no data”). A high count can indicate instances not emitting status-check metrics rather than truly healthy ones.

attributes.description: Number of EC2 instances reporting metrics but with no StatusCheckFailed signal in the latest 5-minute window (shown as “no data”). A high count can indicate instances not emitting status-check metrics rather than truly healthy ones.

query.esql: FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | STATS no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent IS NULL | KEEP no_data

FROM metrics-aws.ec2.otel-* | WHERE InstanceId IS NOT NULL AND InstanceId != "" | INLINE STATS latest_global = MAX(@timestamp) | WHERE DATE_DIFF("minutes", @timestamp, latest_global) <= 5 | STATS failed_recent = MAX(CASE(stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/EC2/StatusCheckFailed`, NULL)) BY InstanceId, cloud.region | STATS no_data = COUNT(*) WHERE failed_recent IS NULL | KEEP no_data
